item.title: Number of govt pry school students decreasing: state minister
item.description: State minister for primary and mass education Bobby Hajjaj on Thursday said that the number of students in the government primary schools was decreasing....

item.title: ‘Criminal’ snatched from police in Lalmonirhat after cop assault
item.description: A group of people believed to be drug traders, snatched away an alleged drug peddler from police custody after physically assaulting two police officers in Hatibandha Upazila of Lalmonirhat on Thursday....

item.title: Four Ctg siblings die in Oman
item.description: The dream of celebrating younger brother’s special day was shattered as four siblings from Rangunia upazila in Chattogram died together in Oman while shopping for his wedding....

item.title: 6 Chinese nationals among 9 held over online gambling, cyber fraud
item.description: The Detective Brach of Dhaka Metropolitan Police has arrested nine individuals, including six Chinese nationals, in connection with their alleged involvement in online gambling, cyber fraud and illegal digital financial transactions in the capital Dhaka....City & Local Guides

item.title: Cleanliness drive begins under PM’s ‘Clean, Green City’ prog
item.description: Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Muktijoddha Projonmo on Thursday launched a cleanliness drive to implement the announcement of prime minister Tarique Rahman’s ‘Clean City, Green City’ programme....

item.title: New law criminalises smuggling of migrants: workshop
item.description: The Bangladesh government’s new legislation aimed at ensuring justice for trafficking survivors has expanded protection and strengthened investigations and prosecutions, government officials and activists said on Thursday at a workshop....

item.title: Auto-rickshaw driver’s throat-slit body recovered
item.description: The police recovered the throat-slit body of an auto-rickshaw driver from the Gazari Forest at Sreepur in Gazipur on Thursday....

item.title: 2 crushed by train while making TikTok video
item.description: Two youths were killed after being run over by a train while attempting to record a TikTok video at Dhirashram Railway Station in Gazipur Sadar upazila Wednesday night....Cultural Event Listings

item.title: 11-year-old girl hit by bullet dies in Ctg
item.description: An 11-year-old schoolgirl, who was hit by a bullet during indiscriminate firing by miscreants at Roufabad Colony under Bayezid Bostami police station in Chattogram city on May 7, died at Chattogram Medical College Hospital on Thursday morning....

item.title: Israel becomes world’s most disliked country: survey
item.description: Israel is now perceived more negatively than any other country in the world, followed by North Korea, Afghanistan and Iran, according to new global polling published by Nira Data as part of its 2026 democracy and country perception research, reports Middle East Monitor....

item.title: Lebanon, Israel hold new talks in US as ceasefire nears end
item.description: Lebanon and Israel held new peace talks in Washington on Thursday, as their latest ceasefire — considered to still be in place despite hundreds of deaths in Israeli strikes — nears its end....

item.title: DB busts fake note producing ring
item.description: The Detective Branch of Dhaka Metropolitan Police has said that they have busted a fake note producing ring in the capital and arrested three of the ring members at places in Dhaka and Gazipur. ...

item.title: Man killed in Narayanganj ‘robber attack’
item.description: A man was killed in an attack allegedly by robbers at Muktishpur village in Sonargaon upazila of Narayanganj early Thursday....

item.title: Quintuplets born at BMU released healthy
item.description: Five premature and low-birth-weight babies born together under the intensive care of doctors at the neonatal intensive care unit of Bangladesh Medical University were discharged healthy from the university in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: ISU holds orientation for new students
item.description: International Standard University organised a fresher’s reception for students of the spring 2026 semester on its campus at Mohakhali in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: Japanese delegation visits Ctg Port
item.description: A delegation led by Japanese ambassador to Bangladesh Saida Shinichi visited the Chittagong Port on Wednesday to discuss strategic infrastructure projects and ways to enhance maritime cooperation between Bangladesh and Japan....

item.title: Nevian exports medicines to Canada
item.description: Nevian Life Science PLC, formerly Novartis Bangladesh Limited, held an event, celebrating a milestone by commencing medicine exports to Canada, at Nevian’s manufacturing plant of Tongi in Gazipur on Wednesday....

item.title: MEASLES DEATHS: CPB demands govt punish those responsible for negligence
item.description: Leaders of the Communist Party of Bangladesh on Thursday called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to identify those in the interim government responsible for negligence and punish them, as more than 400 children have so far died of measles in the country....

item.title: Local polls may start in Oct: EC
item.description: The Election Commission is preparing to start local government elections in October this year, but the exact date remains unconfirmed, EC high officials said, adding that the initial phase might begin with municipality or upazila parishad elections....

item.title: Khalilur pledges to be full-time UNGA president for everyone
item.description: Foreign minister Khalilur Rahman has vowed to serve as a ‘full-time president’ and president for everyone, if elected as the president of the 81st session of the United Nations General Assembly....

item.title: Reclaiming internationalism through Palestine solidarity
item.description: Marxist scholar Nagesh Rao, who teaches English and Modern Languages at Independent University, Bangladesh, discusses in an interview with Saydia Gulrukh of New Age the possibilities of building Palestine solidarity beyond symbolic action......

item.title: Liberal myths confronted in Palestine
item.description: THE history of modern Palestine cannot be understood without beginning with the Nakba — the violence of 1948 that led to the mass displacement of more than 700,000 Palestinians during the creation of Israel. Entire villages were massacred, families scattered across borders, and a people transformed into one of the world’s longest-standing refugee populations. Yet......

item.title: Moral bankruptcy of ‘great powers’
item.description: As another year passes since the 1948 Catastrophe, the displacement of the Palestinian people remains the most glaring wound on the conscience of the international community. For us in Bangladesh, a nation that rose from the ashes of a scorched-earth genocide in 1971, the images emerging from Gaza and the West Bank are not merely news items; they are traumatic......Diaspora Community Forum

item.title: Press freedom paradox
item.description: IN COUNTRIES where democracy becomes increasingly performative, the first institution to lose oxygen is often the press. Not because journalism suddenly becomes irrelevant, but because it becomes too relevant. A frightened state can tolerate corruption, incompetence and even violence for remarkably long periods of time, yet it struggles to tolerate scrutiny. That......

item.title: Climate education is survival imperative
item.description: MANY consider Bangladesh to be the ultimate ‘climate-vulnerable state.’ Rising temperatures are altering the habits of wild animals, intensifying storms, creating chronic heatwaves, exacerbating droughts, and encroaching on coastal lands and freshwater supplies. This crisis is changing lives every day, in every way. Yet, at the same time, Bangladesh’s education......

item.title: Govt needs to amend health system gaps early
item.description: THE shortage of Vitamin A capsules, which surfaced because of complications in the procurement system, has yet to be resolved. This is worrying in that Vitamin A deficiency, along with malnutrition, is said to be partly adding to the high number of children’s deaths caused by measles, confirmed or suspected. The special assistant to the prime minister on health, at a press......

item.title: Structural weaknesses the govt must address
item.description: THE Fitch Ratings, one of the three big credit rating agencies, revising Bangladesh’s sovereign outlook from ‘stable’ to ‘negative’ should serve as a warning for policy-makers. Although the rating agency has kept the country’s long-term foreign currency issuer default rating at ‘B+’, the report signals growing doubts about Bangladesh’s economic resilience and governance......

item.title: Brazil renew Ancelotti contract until 2030
item.description: Carlo Ancelotti will remain the coach of Brazil's national team until 2030, the country's football federation said in a statement Thursday in a vote of confidence before the World Cup....

item.title: Mbappe, Dembele head up France squad for 2026 World Cup
item.description:
Kylian Mbappe will spearhead France at the World Cup after national team coach Didier Deschamps on Thursday announced his 26-man squad for this summer's tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico.
...
